title: perf: audit quick-wins (CI scan prune, what_if grid cap, upload decimation, DB indexes, live-monitor cap)
intent: Implements the confirmed+cheap subset of the read-only PERFORMANCE_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md one-hour fix list. Each change is bounded and backed by a measurement in the audit report. Item 5 (rosette JSON->SQLite store migration) is intentionally deferred to a separate PR due to data-continuity risk.

diff_articulation.what_changed: (1) Two governance tree scanners switched from unpruned SERVICES.rglob('*.py') to an os.walk with in-place PRUNE_DIRS pruning (mirrors scripts/check_dxf_compat.py:iter_python_files), so they no longer descend into services/api/.venv/site-packages; each went from a 120s CI timeout to ~1.5-2.5s. (2) /api/cam/opt/what_if now validates grid as exactly two positive integers and rejects requests above configurable total-cell / cells*moves workload limits, bounding the O(grid*moves) optimizer that ran ~10s at 20x20 without rejecting cheap rectangular grids solely because one axis exceeds 12. (3) /api/cam/sim/upload decimates its returned moves preview to max_moves (default/server cap 5000) unless include_moves=true, reports preview_stride, and preserves first/last preview moves; move_count and stats still reflect the full path. (4) rmos_db adds preventive strip_families + composite (filter, created_at DESC) indexes (SQLite + PG), SCHEMA_VERSION 3->4, and applies v4 indexes as a version-gated migration before recording v4; the migration audit now reads SCHEMA_VERSION instead of a stale hard-coded 1. (5) useLiveMonitorStore and the visible LiveMonitor component both cap retained display events to 500. Tests added for the what_if workload guard, request-schema visibility, upload decimation, and RMOS schema/index migration.
diff_articulation.why_not_redundant: Each item removes a distinct, measured cost path (CI wall-clock, request-thread latency, response payload size, unbounded frontend memory) documented in PERFORMANCE_AUDIT_2026-07-04.md. None is a cosmetic refactor. The API changes preserve defaults and small-request behavior, but they intentionally tighten acceptance for oversized/expensive requests and are covered by new tests.

commands_run: pytest tests/test_cam_what_if_smoke.py -q -> 8 passed (incl. malformed-grid rejection, workload cap, rectangular-grid compatibility, schema text)
pytest tests/test_simulation_endpoint_smoke.py -q -> 31 passed, 8 xfailed (incl. decimation + first/last preview preservation + full-fidelity opt-in + max_moves cap)
pytest tests/test_sqlite_art_stores.py -q -> 21 passed (incl. current schema version + v3->v4 index migration)
git ls-files | rg '(^|/)(\.venv|venv|node_modules|__pycache__|\.git)/.*\.py$' -> no checked-in Python under pruned dependency/cache dirs

files.behavior_change: Scanner no longer descends into dependency dirs; scans same real repo source, ~50x faster. No change to violations reported.
Scanner no longer descends into dependency dirs; scans same real repo source, ~80x faster. No change to violations reported.

files.intent: Cap the /opt/what_if total grid cells and cells*moves workload to bound optimizer cost.
files.behavior_change: Expensive grids now return 400 with a user-facing remediation. Defaults are unchanged; cheap rectangular grids such as 16x4 remain valid; limits are configurable via LTB_WHATIF_MAX_GRID_CELLS and LTB_WHATIF_MAX_WORK_UNITS and documented in the request schema.

files.intent: Add preventive strip_families + composite created_at indexes; bump SCHEMA_VERSION to 4.
files.behavior_change: Additive indexes on SQLite and PG init; v4 preventive indexes are applied as a version-gated migration before schema v4 is recorded. No data migration, no query result change.

files.intent: Keep RMOS migration audit aligned with the declared SCHEMA_VERSION.
files.behavior_change: Audit no longer warns against stale expected schema version 1 after the DB moves to v4.

files.intent: Decimate the /api/cam/sim/upload moves preview to bound payload.
files.behavior_change: Large uploads return a decimated moves preview + moves_returned/moves_decimated/preview_stride fields; max_moves is capped at the server preview limit unless include_moves=true; small uploads and move_count/stats unchanged. The sampled preview preserves first and last moves.

files.intent: Bound the LiveMonitor store event buffer to 500 retained display events.
files.behavior_change: events[] retains only the most recent 500; eventCounts remain true cumulative totals.

files.intent: Apply the same 500-event cap to the visible LiveMonitor component's local event list.
files.behavior_change: The visible event stream retains only the most recent 500 events; category counts remain cumulative.

files.intent: Cover malformed grid rejection, workload cap rejection, rectangular-grid compatibility, and request-schema visibility.
Cover upload decimation + first/last preview preservation + full-fidelity opt-in + small-path passthrough + server cap on max_moves preview.
Cover RMOS schema version and v4 index migration behavior.
The read-only audit report that motivates and measures these fixes.

architecture_scan.acknowledgement_notes: All changes bounded and measured. Defaults/small requests remain compatible, but the two API guards intentionally reject or decimate expensive caller-controlled requests. Item 5 (rosette store migration) deferred to a separate PR for data-continuity review.
